titleStmt.title: SII 27.95: original edition by M. D. Sampath, H. K. Narasimhamurthy, Madhav N. Katti and B. R. Gopal
No. 95. (A.R. No. 228 of 1931-32.) PEDDAVEḶAGAṬŪRU, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.

summary.p: Pillar lying in the field in front of the Varadarājasvāmin temple.

This is dated in the cyclic year Sarvvadhāri, Kārttika śu. 1, Sunday. On palaeographical grounds the record may be assigned to the 15th century. As maṇḍlēśvara Vijaya-bhūpati is identical with Vijaya (Bukka III), son of Dēvarāya I, the Vijayanagara king, the intended date of the record would be 1408 A.D., October 28, when the weekday, however, was Saturday.

It registers the gift of the village Hiriya-veḷagaṁṭūru in Velumālinanāḍu as sarvamānya by the prince for the daily offerings and worship of the god Mallikārjunadēva and of the goddess Bhramarādēvi of Śrīparavata and for feeding daily ten brāhmaṇas thereat.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
sarvvadhāri saṁ
vatsarada kārti
ka su 1 Ā śrī maṁḍa
ḷēśvara śrī vīra vija
ya bhūpati rā
ya Oḍeyaru vē
ludūlina nāḍa hi
riya veḷaṁgāṭū
ranu śrīparvvatada ma
lli kārjjuna dēvari
ge divya paṁcāmri
takeyu śrī rudrā
bhiśēkha nayivēdya
muṁtāda śōḍa
ṣōpacārakke brama
rā dēviya nayi

vēdya muṁtāda
śōḍaṣōpacāra
kkeyu Alli nitya
U hattumaṁ
di brāhmarige
chatrava nikkuvada
kkeU Aṣṭabhōga
tējasvāṁmya sa
hita sarvamāṁnya
vāgi niṁma hiri
ya veḷaṁgāṭūra
nu dhārāpūrvvaka vā
gi koṭeU yi dha
rṁmmakke tappida
varu kavi
leya koṁda pāLines 32-34 are engraved on
padali hōha
ru
Digital edition of SII 27.95 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
